---
title: "Resume"
---

## David T. Watkeys

Roanoke, VA | davwat22@vt.edu

---

History graduate student and U.S. Navy veteran specializing in Indigenous 
sovereignty, the history of the War on Drugs, legal history, and 
twentieth-century U.S. environmental and cultural history. Experienced in 
leadership, consultation, archival research, scholarly editing, database 
construction, accessibility software, and developing public-facing historical 
communication.

---

## Education

**Master of Arts in History** — Expected May 2026
Virginia Tech | Blacksburg, VA
GPA: 3.91 | Funded Fellowship | In Progress

**Bachelor of Arts in History** — 2024
Virginia Tech | Blacksburg, VA
GPA: 3.787 | Magna Cum Laude

**Associate of Science** — 2022
Virginia Western Community College | Roanoke, VA
GPA: 3.756 | Magna Cum Laude

---

## Professional Experience

**Research & Exhibit Development Consultant**
Salem Museum, Salem, VA | Jun–Aug 2025

- Collaborated with archaeologists and museum staff to research and develop 
a 1,500 sq. ft. physical exhibit documenting Tutelo and Monacan cultural 
artifacts recovered from a 1990s excavation of the Moyer softball fields
- Contributed to a parallel exhibit presenting a timeline of Indigenous 
history in the Roanoke Valley region

**Research Assistant**
Virginia Tech Library, Blacksburg, VA | 2024–Present

- Built a searchable primary-source database of approximately 250 datafied 
sources to support faculty and student research at Virginia Tech, in 
collaboration with Professor Bruce Pencek

**Graduate Editor**
Virginia Tech Undergraduate Historical Review | 2024–Present

- Edited and shepherded five articles through peer-reviewed undergraduate 
journal publication, with four more in production
- Recruited and led a team of eight undergraduate and one graduate editorial assistant
